signals.headline: Bitcoin Slips Below $71,000 Following Trump Blockade Order | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Bitcoin Slips Below $71,000 Following Trump Blockade Order | YES | HIGH confidence**

Bitcoin declined below $71,000 as Trump ordered a blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, adding to weekend losses.[5] As of April 13, Bitcoin was trading at $71,188.84.[6]

signals.headline: Iran Ceasefire Collapsed, Oil Prices Spiked | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Iran Ceasefire Collapsed, Oil Prices Spiked | YES | HIGH confidence**

A two-week U.S.-Iran ceasefire collapsed on April 9, triggering crude oil price increases and forcing $427 million in crypto short positions into liquidation, causing Bitcoin to trade at $71,362 with a 4.5% intraday gain.[8]

signals.headline: South Korea Inflation at 2
signals.raw:
South Korea Inflation at 2.2% in March 2026, Below Forecasts | YES | HIGH confidence**  
South Korea's annual inflation rose to 2.2% in March 2026 from 2.0%, missing market expectations of 2.4%, per April data release[2][4]. This follows steady 2% readings in Jan-Feb, contained pre-Iran volatility, potentially easing BoK rate cut bets despite quarterly forecasts of 2.6%[2].

signals.raw:
Germany Inflation Surge to 2.7% in March 2026 | YES | HIGH confidence**  
Germany's annual inflation rate jumped to 2.7% in March 2026 from 1.9% prior, as reported on April 15[4], aligning with the ifo Spring 2026 forecast of energy-driven pressures pushing rates to 2.8% annually amid the Iran war shock[1]. signals.headline: Germany GDP Forecast Cut to 0
signals.raw:
Germany GDP Forecast Cut to 0.6% Growth in 2026 | YES | MED confidence**  
ifo's Joint Economic Forecast Spring 2026 (April 1) slashed Germany's 2026 GDP growth to 0.6% from prior estimates (down 0.6pp), citing energy shocks overshadowing stimulus, with 0.9% in 2027[1]. No South Korea GDP updates; IMF projects $1.93T nominal[5][8]. Could impact markets on Eurozone recession risks.
